---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: c6be85bf7a5fa7448a4ac7eb01f3030aad02f6a4
findings:
  - { id: judged-ac2-echoed-doubled-id-still-doubles, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's letter — 'a freshly minted judged finding id carries exactly one judged- prefix' — is not guaranteed by judgedFindingID (internal/align/judge.go:201-208). The helper is idempotent against ONE pre-existing prefix (HasPrefix short-circuit), not normalizing: on the exact regeneration path the trap describes, if the id echoed back to the judge is an ARCHIVED pre-fix doubled id ('judged-judged-x'), the freshly minted finding preserves both prefixes verbatim. Preserving may in fact be the better choice (collapsing to 'judged-x' would sever the archived disposition's id join, the very continuity ac-2's second clause protects), but that means the AC's 'exactly one prefix' letter and its 'dispositions reference ids exactly as minted' spirit conflict for this input class, and the implementation resolved the ambiguity silently — no test pins judgedFindingID('judged-judged-x'), and nothing in PLAN.md §7 records the choice, contrary to the invention-ledger discipline. (confidence 0.45)" }
  - { id: judged-ac2-roundtrip-pin-has-no-encode-leg, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's 'still decodes and round-trips completely untouched' is only half-pinned: TestArchivedDoubledJudgedID_DecodesAndRoundTripsUntouched (internal/align/judged_test.go) decodes the same constant fixture bytes twice and compares the two decodes — a near-tautological stability check. No leg re-encodes the decoded report (or drives the real regeneration/carry write path) and proves the doubled id survives back onto disk byte-for-byte, which is what 'round-trips' means and what actually protects an archived report the tooling later rewrites. The prospective-only claim ('nothing on the decode path touches Finding.ID') is asserted in comments and by decode-purity, but the write-back direction of the round-trip is disclosed-as-unproven at best. (confidence 0.55)" }
  - { id: judged-ac4-pinned-fragment-entry-silently-unpinned, kind: judged, text: "ac-4 declares the fragment grammar as spec/\u003cname\u003e#\u003cac-id\u003e, but Binding.Validate (internal/artifact/bindings.go) admits the pinned form spec/\u003cname\u003e@\u003ccommit\u003e#\u003cac-id\u003e (it checks only Kind==spec \u0026\u0026 Fragment()), and ResolveBindingAC then silently drops the pin (returns Ref{Kind,Name} only). VL-003's new cross-check therefore validates a pinned entry's AC id against the CURRENT committed spec rather than the pinned revision, and never reds the out-of-grammar form itself — the pin is neither honored nor rejected. A bindings author who pins an entry to a revision whose AC set differs from HEAD gets a green (or red) verdict about the wrong revision with no finding disclosing the discrepancy, contrary to the fail-closed posture the rest of VL-003 (and the 'unknown enum values fail closed' ground rule) takes. (confidence 0.35)" }
digest: sha256:0e642b13e380a7bac34bb5eb8afb0e8ded44e22012b65a9f24342e69820ae823
integrity: sha256:61449b3aa7f15e6c28dcadbc4157062d3583f06aab627b0fa52b5b27ec7e7c71
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"ac2-echoed-doubled-id-still-doubles\",\"text\":\"ac-2's letter — 'a freshly minted judged finding id carries exactly one judged- prefix' — is not guaranteed by judgedFindingID (internal/align/judge.go:201-208). The helper is idempotent against ONE pre-existing prefix (HasPrefix short-circuit), not normalizing: on the exact regeneration path the trap describes, if the id echoed back to the judge is an ARCHIVED pre-fix doubled id ('judged-judged-x'), the freshly minted finding preserves both prefixes verbatim. Preserving may in fact be the better choice (collapsing to 'judged-x' would sever the archived disposition's id join, the very continuity ac-2's second clause protects), but that means the AC's 'exactly one prefix' letter and its 'dispositions reference ids exactly as minted' spirit conflict for this input class, and the implementation resolved the ambiguity silently — no test pins judgedFindingID('judged-judged-x'), and nothing in PLAN.md §7 records the choice, contrary to the invention-ledger discipline.\",\"confidence\":0.45},{\"id\":\"ac2-roundtrip-pin-has-no-encode-leg\",\"text\":\"ac-2's 'still decodes and round-trips completely untouched' is only half-pinned: TestArchivedDoubledJudgedID_DecodesAndRoundTripsUntouched (internal/align/judged_test.go) decodes the same constant fixture bytes twice and compares the two decodes — a near-tautological stability check. No leg re-encodes the decoded report (or drives the real regeneration/carry write path) and proves the doubled id survives back onto disk byte-for-byte, which is what 'round-trips' means and what actually protects an archived report the tooling later rewrites. The prospective-only claim ('nothing on the decode path touches Finding.ID') is asserted in comments and by decode-purity, but the write-back direction of the round-trip is disclosed-as-unproven at best.\",\"confidence\":0.55},{\"id\":\"ac4-pinned-fragment-entry-silently-unpinned\",\"text\":\"ac-4 declares the fragment grammar as spec/\u003cname\u003e#\u003cac-id\u003e, but Binding.Validate (internal/artifact/bindings.go) admits the pinned form spec/\u003cname\u003e@\u003ccommit\u003e#\u003cac-id\u003e (it checks only Kind==spec \u0026\u0026 Fragment()), and ResolveBindingAC then silently drops the pin (returns Ref{Kind,Name} only). VL-003's new cross-check therefore validates a pinned entry's AC id against the CURRENT committed spec rather than the pinned revision, and never reds the out-of-grammar form itself — the pin is neither honored nor rejected. A bindings author who pins an entry to a revision whose AC set differs from HEAD gets a green (or red) verdict about the wrong revision with no finding disclosing the discrepancy, contrary to the fail-closed posture the rest of VL-003 (and the 'unknown enum values fail closed' ground rule) takes.\",\"confidence\":0.35}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/ritual-traps@c6be85bf7a5fa7448a4ac7eb01f3030aad02f6a4, spec/ritual-traps@931e3b40be1d375d297b08128443c37e46c93bbd], digest: sha256:0e642b13e380a7bac34bb5eb8afb0e8ded44e22012b65a9f24342e69820ae823, integrity: sha256:61449b3aa7f15e6c28dcadbc4157062d3583f06aab627b0fa52b5b27ec7e7c71, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-ac2-echoed-doubled-id-still-doubles** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-2's letter — 'a freshly minted judged finding id carries exactly one judged- prefix' — is not guaranteed by judgedFindingID (internal/align/judge.go:201-208). The helper is idempotent against ONE pre-existing prefix (HasPrefix short-circuit), not normalizing: on the exact regeneration path the trap describes, if the id echoed back to the judge is an ARCHIVED pre-fix doubled id ('judged-judged-x'), the freshly minted finding preserves both prefixes verbatim. Preserving may in fact be the better choice (collapsing to 'judged-x' would sever the archived disposition's id join, the very continuity ac-2's second clause protects), but that means the AC's 'exactly one prefix' letter and its 'dispositions reference ids exactly as minted' spirit conflict for this input class, and the implementation resolved the ambiguity silently — no test pins judgedFindingID('judged-judged-x'), and nothing in PLAN.md §7 records the choice, contrary to the invention-ledger discipline. (confidence 0.45)
- **judged-ac2-roundtrip-pin-has-no-encode-leg** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-2's 'still decodes and round-trips completely untouched' is only half-pinned: TestArchivedDoubledJudgedID_DecodesAndRoundTripsUntouched (internal/align/judged_test.go) decodes the same constant fixture bytes twice and compares the two decodes — a near-tautological stability check. No leg re-encodes the decoded report (or drives the real regeneration/carry write path) and proves the doubled id survives back onto disk byte-for-byte, which is what 'round-trips' means and what actually protects an archived report the tooling later rewrites. The prospective-only claim ('nothing on the decode path touches Finding.ID') is asserted in comments and by decode-purity, but the write-back direction of the round-trip is disclosed-as-unproven at best. (confidence 0.55)
- **judged-ac4-pinned-fragment-entry-silently-unpinned** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-4 declares the fragment grammar as spec/<name>#<ac-id>, but Binding.Validate (internal/artifact/bindings.go) admits the pinned form spec/<name>@<commit>#<ac-id> (it checks only Kind==spec && Fragment()), and ResolveBindingAC then silently drops the pin (returns Ref{Kind,Name} only). VL-003's new cross-check therefore validates a pinned entry's AC id against the CURRENT committed spec rather than the pinned revision, and never reds the out-of-grammar form itself — the pin is neither honored nor rejected. A bindings author who pins an entry to a revision whose AC set differs from HEAD gets a green (or red) verdict about the wrong revision with no finding disclosing the discrepancy, contrary to the fail-closed posture the rest of VL-003 (and the 'unknown enum values fail closed' ground rule) takes. (confidence 0.35)
